source.title: Hippocampal place-cell sequences depict future paths to remembered goals.

confidence_rationale: Direct in-vivo demonstration that hippocampal place-cell sequences emit goal-biased trajectory candidates before goal-directed navigation. Mapping fidelity is high for MECH-057b's claim that hippocampal sequence completion (here: full goal-reaching trajectory generation) is what makes a candidate eligible for downstream selection. Source quality is exceptional (Nature, large rodent ensemble, novel start/goal pairs as a control). Transfer risk is moderate: rats in an open arena with a single goal type, and the read-out structure (BG / E3) is inferred rather than measured here.
failure_signatures: If only fragmentary sequences (not goal-reaching) appeared, MECH-057b's gate-on-completion framing would be weakened.
If sequence emission were equally biased on novel-start-and-novel-goal pairs that the animal had never solved, the inference that completion verification gates candidacy would be undermined.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Before goal-directed navigation in an open arena, the rat hippocampus generates brief sequences encoding spatial trajectories strongly biased to progress from current location to a known goal location, and these sequences predict immediate future behaviour.
mapping.ree_translation: Hippocampal trajectory candidates that successfully terminate at a remembered goal (i.e. complete a goal-reaching sequence) are emitted preferentially. In REE terms, this is the candidacy gate: only sequences that can be 'completed' from current state to a stored goal are promoted to E3 evaluation. The sequence-completion check happens inside the hippocampal substrate, before downstream BG/cortical selection.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The paper measures sequence content and predictive value, not the gate itself. We are inferring that incomplete or non-goal-reaching sequences are suppressed because the recorded population is dominated by goal-reaching sequences -- but absence of evidence for fragmentary emission is not direct evidence for suppression. A V3 instrumentation experiment must measure the suppression directly.
mapping.source_context: Awake rats, open arena, multiple goal locations, hippocampal CA1 ensemble recording during pause-then-run epochs.

confidence_components.notes: High source quality (Nature, large ensemble, well-controlled novel start/goal manipulation). Mapping is good but the gating mechanism is inferred rather than dissected. Transfer risk reflects rodent-to-REE substrate generalization.
